what_it_does: Parses every Markdown resource entry in a UI Contract corpus, enforces a frozen contract-format rule set (V1–V21 static rules plus a structural subset of liveness checks), collects violations into tiered hard/soft findings, and exits non-zero when any enforced rule is broken. Reality rules V22–V26 are skipped by default and only fail-loud when --include-reality is set without generated artifacts.
observations: Uses stdlib plus PyYAML and optionally jsonschema for JSON Schema 2020-12 metaschema checks.
A Report class accumulates hard/soft violations and skipped rules, then drives the final pass/fail decision.
A Corpus class loads TRANSPORTS.md, CONVENTIONS.md, atlas files, journeys, and all resources/*.md entries into indexes.
Rule functions are named rule_V* and correspond to sections of the contract format.
Frontmatter is split and parsed as YAML; op sections are discovered via ## op: headers; fences are extracted with regex line scanning.
Validates uniform verbs, status/liveness/direction enums, binding kinds, transport declarations, exposure references, wikilink targets, scheme ownership, atlas coverage, and line budgets.
Reality rules are explicitly reported as SKIPPED-NEEDS-REALITY rather than silently passed.
Main prints grouped hard and soft findings, stats, and skipped-rule notes, then exits 1 if any violation exists.
standouts: Fail-loud design: malformed entries must fail and unbuilt reality checks are not falsely green.
Tiered severity model separates hard structural breaks from soft proxy/lint reflexes.
Honest status handling for planned schemes and unbuilt live-system artifacts.
Corpus-wide uniqueness checks for basenames, error codes, and op restatement lines.
conventions: Follows the argparse-driven main() script pattern.
Keeps format-specific constants at the top and maps them to contract enums.
Rule functions mutate a shared Report object passed alongside a Corpus object.
Uses regex-based Markdown parsing rather than a formal Markdown AST.
Mixes small classes with imperative rule functions and nested helpers inside main.
concerns: Fence extraction and strip_fences rely on regex line matching that can mishandle nested or indented fences.
parse_kv_block is tolerant and may silently ignore malformed lines in error/example fences.
named_acts extraction from CONVENTIONS.md is heuristic and over-inclusive.
V4 error-code uniqueness splits on '|', assuming a specific inline format that may be brittle.
V7 planned-scheme whitelist is hard-coded rather than derived from a schema.
Nested relpath and render helpers inside main reduce testability.
Several rules duplicate traversal and fence-extraction logic.
notes: A contract-format linter that prefers honest failure over false-green, enforcing static checks now while deferring live-system reality checks until their artifacts exist.
questions: What is the exact expected shape of a contract:op fence beyond the fields currently checked?
How should frontmatter schemes relate to x-scheme declarations inside schemas?
What is the intended resolution semantics for exposure.json# references?
How should the validator behave when --include-reality is set and reality.json exists but is malformed?
Are soft findings intended to block CI or only to produce warnings?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring and implementation both describe a static contract corpus validator that skips reality rules by default and reports violations.
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce a frozen, machine-readable UI contract format while remaining transparent about which checks require a live system, so the ledger cannot be falsely marked green.
novelty: Explicit SKIPPED-NEEDS-REALITY reporting for unbuilt reality artifacts instead of silently passing or falsely failing them by default.
contribution: Provides a concrete, rule-by-rule static validator and violation reporter that a fused system can reuse to keep contract entries consistent with TRANSPORTS, CONVENTIONS, atlas, and journey namespaces.
summary_for_embedding: A Python CLI validator that parses a Markdown-based UI Contract corpus and enforces static format rules (frontmatter, op fences, schemas, references, liveness, atlas coverage), reporting hard and soft violations while deferring reality rules until live artifacts exist.
intention_for_embedding: To give the UI Contract corpus a fail-loud, honest linter that blocks malformed entries without pretending unbuilt live-system checks have passed.

symbols.does: Collects validation violations, skipped rules, and stats.
symbols.description: Maintains lists of hard/soft violations and skipped rules, provides helper methods to record findings, and exposes an ok() predicate for the final exit decision.

symbols.does: Initializes empty violation, skipped, and stats containers.
symbols.description: Sets up the internal state used throughout validation: an empty violations list, an empty skipped list, and a stats dictionary.

symbols.does: Records a rule violation with file location and severity.
symbols.description: Appends a tuple containing rule id, file path, locator, message, and severity to the violations list; hard severity indicates structural breaks while soft indicates proxy/lint reflexes.

symbols.does: Records a rule that was skipped and why.
symbols.description: Appends a rule identifier and explanatory reason to the skipped list, used primarily for reality rules that need live artifacts.

symbols.does: Returns true when no violations were recorded.
symbols.description: Simple predicate returning whether the violations list is empty; the driver uses this to decide between pass and fail exit codes.

symbols.does: Parses YAML frontmatter and returns the remainder of the file body.
symbols.description: Expects a leading --- frontmatter block, validates its closure, parses it with yaml.safe_load, and reports V1 failures when the block is missing, malformed, or not a mapping.

symbols.does: Yields fenced code blocks from Markdown body with line numbers.
symbols.description: Scans line by line for triple-backtick or triple-tilde fences, captures the info string and content, and reports the 1-based starting line of each fence relative to the body.

symbols.does: Splits the body into sections headed by ## op: and extracts their fences.
symbols.description: Finds H2 headers, isolates each op section until the next H2 or EOF, then extracts contract:op, error, schema, example, and fsm fences while recording line numbers.

symbols.does: Parses simple key-value lines from error/example fences.
symbols.description: Reads a fence line by line, treating the first colon on a non-indented line as a key-value separator; used for contract:error and contract:example blocks.

symbols.does: Represents the loaded contract corpus and its derived indexes.
symbols.description: Holds the corpus root, resources directory, parsed entries, basenames, scheme ownership, transports, named acts, atlas affordances, journeys, op names, and restatement tracking.

symbols.does: Initializes empty corpus indexes from a root path.
symbols.description: Stores the root path, computes the resources directory, and prepares empty dictionaries and sets for entries, basenames, schemes, transports, acts, atlas data, journeys, op names, and restatements.

symbols.does: Loads structural files and all resource entries into indexes.
symbols.description: Reads TRANSPORTS.md, CONVENTIONS.md, atlas files, and journey files to populate reference sets, then parses every resources/*.md file into entries, recording frontmatter, ops, and scheme ownership while checking basename uniqueness.

symbols.does: Validates required frontmatter keys and status values.
symbols.description: Checks each entry's YAML frontmatter for required keys, a valid status enum value, a non-empty summary, a list-shaped relates-to, and consistency between the resource key and filename basename.

symbols.does: Validates each op's contract:op fence contents.
symbols.description: Ensures every op section has a valid YAML contract:op fence, that op/resource names match, and that kind, status, direction, liveness, tasks, named acts, and bindings conform to the allowed sets and shapes.

symbols.does: Validates binding kinds, transports, and exposure references.
symbols.description: Checks each binding's kind against BINDING_KINDS, resolves or requires a transport (including mcp server mapping), verifies the transport is declared in TRANSPORTS.md, and ensures exposure is either an exposure.json# key or an explicit n/a string.

symbols.does: Validates contract:schema fences as JSON Schema.
symbols.description: Collects all contract:schema fences in an entry, parses them as JSON, and when jsonschema is available checks them against the Draft 2020-12 metaschema.

symbols.does: Validates error fences for envelope, uniqueness, teach refs, and state coverage.
symbols.description: Parses contract:error fences, requires a code and a teach recovery reference, enforces corpus-wide uniqueness of error codes, and checks that absence-shaped expectations name evidence.

symbols.does: Lints prose for banned UI vocabulary.
symbols.description: Strips fences from op sections and scans remaining prose for purpose-free banned words and phrases, honoring lint-ok escapes and reporting soft findings.

symbols.does: Removes fenced code blocks from Markdown text.
symbols.description: Iterates lines, detects triple-backtick or triple-tilde fence markers, skips lines until the matching closing marker, and returns the remaining prose.

symbols.does: Resolves wikilink targets against the corpus namespace.
symbols.description: Scans each entry's body and frontmatter relates-to for [[...]] links, strips anchors and aliases, and reports unknown targets that are not resource, journey, or structural file basenames.

symbols.does: Checks that used schemes have owning entries.
symbols.description: Collects x-scheme and scheme references across entries, compares them against frontmatter-declared scheme owners, and treats documented planned schemes as skipped rather than hard failures.

symbols.does: Enforces restatement-first and line budgets.
symbols.description: Requires each op section to begin with a bold restatement, ensures restatement lines are corpus-unique, and checks per-file and per-section line budgets.

symbols.does: Validates write-op consequence and emit proof shapes.
symbols.description: For act/create/update/delete ops, requires at least one of emits or consequences, checks each consequence row has when and expect, and requires bound or evidence for non-absence expectations.

symbols.does: Checks liveness-specific documentation completeness.
symbols.description: For snapshot liveness requires a live-twin field; for watch/duplex/binary-stream requires frames, resume, keepalive, and termination documentation.

symbols.does: Validates atlas affordance coverage in both directions.
symbols.description: Ensures every affordance referenced in an op fence is defined in FEATURE-ATLAS.md and that every defined affordance is either reached by an op or listed in OUT-OF-SCOPE.md.

symbols.does: Checks consistency between entry frontmatter status and op fence statuses.
symbols.description: Compares the entry's declared status against the most mature op fence status, flagging building entries without building/live ops and planned entries containing building/live ops.

symbols.does: Reports reality rules as skipped or fail-loud when requested.
symbols.description: Lists V22–V26 as needing reality.json and evidence artifacts; skips them by default and only fails if --include-reality is set while the artifacts are still missing.

symbols.does: Drives argument parsing, rule execution, and report output.
symbols.description: Parses --corpus and --include-reality flags, loads the corpus, runs all static rules plus reality-rule handling, then prints stats, skipped rules, grouped hard/soft violations, and exits with 0 or 1.

symbols.does: Computes a path relative to the corpus root for display.
symbols.description: Nested helper inside main that attempts to return a project-relative path string, falling back to the original path if relativization fails.

symbols.does: Prints grouped violations by rule.
symbols.description: Nested helper inside main that groups a list of violation tuples by rule id, prints each group with file and locator context, and is used separately for hard and soft findings.
